English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
New York State College of Veterinary Medicine oral history project
no primary creator
Baker, Donald Wyckoff, 1899-
Calnek, B. W.
Cole, Randall Knight, 1912-
Dukes, H. H. (Henry Hugh), 1895-
Erdheim, Morris.
Ettinger, Stephen J.
Fabricant, Julius, 1919-
Ferber, Robert.
Fincher, Myron G.
Habel, Robert E.
Levine, Philip P.
Logue, Jeanne.
Logue, Joseph.
Sager, Floyd, 1894-
Scott, Fredric W.
Wasserman, R. H. (Robert Harold), 1926-
Williams, Luella.
Winter, A. J. (Alexander J.), 1931-
Woelffer, Elmer.
Leonard, Ellis P., interviewer.
Finkel, Laura, interviewer.
Griffith, Jeanne, interviewer.
Whitaker, Susanne, interviewer.
24-4-3349
.4 cubic feet. .4 cubic feet.
1972-2004. audio tapes, cd's. Recordings of College of Veterinary Medicine events and individual oral history interviews. Collection material in English <<<<<<< HEAD 1 - trans. 239a 1 - trans. 239b 1 - trans. 239c ======= 1 - trans. 239a 1 - trans. 239b 1 - trans. 239c >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab 5491241
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
Laura Finkel
Date completed:
07/18/2007
EAD encoding:
Laura Finkel, 2004
ORGANIZATIONAL HISTORY
Oral history project begun in 1973 by Ellis P. Leonard for use when writing history of the New York State College of Veterinary Medicine.
COLLECTION ARRANGEMENT
each genre filed together
SERIES LIST
Series I. AUDIO TAPES Box 1
Series II. CD MASTER COPIES Box 1
Series III. CD USE COPIES Box 1
Available Copies:
Audio cassette tapes
CD's
Cite As: <<<<<<< HEAD
New York State College of Veterinary Medicine Oral History Project, #24:aspace_fragment_e9931f0eabd19594ea6f2a217998d4694349. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library. =======
New York State College of Veterinary Medicine Oral History Project, #24:aspace_fragment_dc2a806d86ec68e270fa42027e9767484349. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library. >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
Custodial History Recordings made by Ellis P. Leonard; Suzanne Whitaker; and Laura Finkel and Jeannie Griffith
PROVENANCE
Recordings made by Ellis P. Leonard; Suzanne Whitaker; and Laura Finkel and Jeannie Griffith
Scope and content
The project includes 25 audio cassettes, 26 master copy CDs, 26 service copy CDs, 3 reel-to-reel tapes, and 3 transcripts.These interviews were conducted with veterinarians, faculty of the NYS College of Veterinary Medicine, and others regarding the development of veterinary medicine and practice in the United States as well as recollections of events in the history of the NYS College of Veterinary Medicine at Cornell University.
Scope and content
Interviews were originally done by Ellis P. Leonard, Emeritus Professor of Small Animal Surgery, beginning in 1972, for his use in writing a history of the New York State College of Veterinary Medicine. Leonard interviewed Donald W. Baker, August 27, 1975; Arthur Gordon Danks, August 6, 1979; H. H. Dukes, August 8, 1973 (original tape and transcript available); Myron G. Fincher, June 8, 1972 (original tape and transcript only); Philip P. Levine, 1978; Floyd C. Sager, January 24, 1974 (original tape and transcript available); Luella Williams, ca. 1975; and Elmer Woelfer, September 22, 1978.
Scope and content
In 2004, the College initiated a second project. Laura Finkel and Jeannie Griffith interviewed Bruce Calnak, March 18, 2004 (2 tapes); Randall Cole, March 19, 2004;Julius Fabricant, April 8, 2004 (2 tapes); Robert Habel, April 16, 2004 (2 tapes); George Poppensiek, April 30, 2004 (2 tapes); Fred Scott, May 28, 2004; Robert Wasserman, May 7, 2004. Additionally, Laura Finkel and Susanne Whitaker interviewed Alexander Winter on April 29, 2004. At Reunion, June 11, 2004, Laura Finkel interviewed Morris Erdheim, Robert Farber, and Jeanne and Joe Logue.
Scope and content
There are also recordings of the College of Veterinary Medicine Alumni Association meeting in 1985, recorded by Ellis Leonard, and of a lecture by Stephen Ettinger, "The Five Veterinarians I Met on the Way to Heaven," April 16, 2004.
CD-ROMS. CD-Rs, Audiocassettes. Veterinary medicine -- Study and teaching.
